framework_name: CIS Amazon Web Services Foundations Benchmark
disclaimer: This report assists with compliance assessment against CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark v3.0.0. It does not constitute official CIS certification. 7 of 62 recommendations require manual review and are not automatable.

1.1.title: Maintain current contact details
1.1.description: Ensure contact email and phone details associated with the AWS account are current and map to more than one individual.
1.1.manual_steps: Sign in to AWS Console > Account Settings > verify contact details are current and map to a distribution list or multiple individuals.
1.1.evidence_template: Control 1.1 requires manual verification. Check AWS account contact details in the AWS Console.

1.2.title: Ensure security contact information is registered
1.2.description: AWS provides customers with the option to register a security-specific point of contact. The alternate security contact will receive security-related notifications.
1.2.manual_steps: Verify the security contact email is monitored and reaches the appropriate security team.
1.2.evidence_template: Security contact registration was checked via AWS Account API. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.3.title: Ensure security questions are registered in the AWS account
1.3.description: Security questions provide an additional layer of identity verification during account recovery.
1.3.manual_steps: Sign in as root > Account Settings > Configure Security Challenge Questions.
1.3.evidence_template: Control 1.3 requires manual verification via AWS Console.

1.4.title: Ensure no 'root' user account access key exists
1.4.description: The root account has unrestricted access to all AWS resources. Access keys provide programmatic access. Root access keys should be deleted to prevent their use.
1.4.evidence_template: Root account access key existence was verified via IAM GetAccountSummary (AccountAccessKeysPresent). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.5.title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the 'root' user account
1.5.description: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password.
1.5.evidence_template: Root MFA status was verified via IAM GetAccountSummary (AccountMFAEnabled). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.6.title: Ensure hardware MFA is enabled for the 'root' user account
1.6.description: Hardware MFA devices provide a higher level of protection than virtual MFA because they cannot be intercepted by phishing or malware on the authenticating device.
1.6.manual_steps: Verify the MFA device serial number does not contain 'mfa/root-account-mfa-device' (which indicates virtual MFA).
1.6.evidence_template: Root MFA device type was verified via IAM ListVirtualMFADevices. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.7.title: Eliminate use of the 'root' user for administrative and daily tasks
1.7.description: The root account should only be used for initial account setup and rare administrative tasks that require root access.
1.7.manual_steps: Review IAM Credential Report: verify root 'password_last_used' and 'access_key_1_last_used_date' are not recent (>90 days or N/A).
1.7.evidence_template: Control 1.7 requires manual review of root account usage via IAM Credential Report.

1.8.title: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
1.8.description: Password policies enforce complexity requirements. A minimum length of 14 characters significantly increases brute force resistance.
1.8.evidence_template: IAM password policy was verified via GetAccountPasswordPolicy. MinimumPasswordLength checked against >= 14. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.9.title: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse
1.9.description: Password reuse prevention set to 24 or greater ensures users cannot cycle back to recently used passwords.
1.9.evidence_template: IAM password policy PasswordReusePrevention checked against >= 24. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.10.title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password
1.10.description: All IAM users with console access should have MFA enabled to protect against credential theft.
1.10.evidence_template: MFA status was verified for all IAM users with console passwords via GetLoginProfile and ListMFADevices. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.11.title: Do not setup access keys during initial user setup
1.11.description: Avoid creating access keys during initial user creation. Keys should only be created when there is a specific programmatic access requirement.
1.11.manual_steps: Review IAM Credential Report: for users with password_enabled=true, verify access_key_1_active was not created at the same time as the user.
1.11.evidence_template: Control 1.11 requires manual review of IAM Credential Report.

1.12.title: Ensure credentials unused for 45 days or greater are disabled
1.12.description: Credentials (passwords and access keys) unused for 45+ days should be disabled to reduce the attack surface.
1.12.evidence_template: Access key last-used dates were verified via GetAccessKeyLastUsed. Threshold: 45 days. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.13.title: Ensure there is only one active access key available for any single IAM user
1.13.description: Each IAM user should have at most one active access key to simplify key management and reduce the window of exposure.
1.13.evidence_template: Active access key count was verified per IAM user via ListAccessKeys. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.14.title: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less
1.14.description: Regular key rotation limits the window of exposure if a key is compromised.
1.14.evidence_template: Access key creation dates were verified via IAM Credential Report. Threshold: 90 days. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.15.title: Ensure IAM Users Receive Permissions Only Through Groups
1.15.description: IAM users should inherit permissions from groups, not have policies attached directly. This enables centralized permission management.
1.15.evidence_template: Direct policy attachments were checked per IAM user via ListUserPolicies and ListAttachedUserPolicies. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.16.title: Ensure IAM policies that allow full '*:*' administrative privileges are not attached
1.16.description: IAM policies with Action:* and Resource:* grant unrestricted access. These should not be attached to any IAM entity.
1.16.evidence_template: IAM policies were scanned for Action:*/Resource:* statements via GetPolicyVersion. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.17.title: Ensure a support role has been created to manage incidents with AWS Support
1.17.description: The AWSSupportAccess managed policy should be attached to at least one IAM role to enable incident management through AWS Support.
1.17.evidence_template: AWSSupportAccess policy attachment was verified via ListEntitiesForPolicy. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.18.title: Ensure IAM instance roles are used for AWS resource access from instances
1.18.description: EC2 instances should use IAM instance profiles (roles) instead of long-lived access keys for AWS API access.
1.18.evidence_template: Running EC2 instances were checked for IAM instance profile attachment via DescribeInstances. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.19.title: Ensure that all the expired SSL/TLS certificates stored in AWS IAM are removed
1.19.description: Expired certificates in IAM should be removed to prevent accidental use and reduce the credential surface.
1.19.evidence_template: SSL/TLS certificates in IAM were checked for expiration via ListServerCertificates. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.20.title: Ensure that IAM Access Analyzer is enabled for all regions
1.20.description: IAM Access Analyzer identifies resources shared with external entities. It should be enabled in all regions to detect unintended public or cross-account access.
1.20.evidence_template: IAM Access Analyzer status was verified per region via ListAnalyzers. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

1.21.title: Ensure IAM users are managed centrally via identity federation or AWS Organizations
1.21.description: For multi-account environments, IAM users should be managed centrally via AWS Organizations, IAM Identity Center, or external IdP federation.
1.21.manual_steps: Verify that AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) or external IdP federation is configured. Check that individual IAM users in member accounts are minimized.
1.21.evidence_template: Control 1.21 requires manual review of identity federation setup.

1.22.title: Ensure access to AWSCloudShellFullAccess is restricted
1.22.description: AWSCloudShellFullAccess allows file upload/download in CloudShell which could be used for data exfiltration. Restrict to a custom policy.
1.22.manual_steps: Verify no IAM entities have AWSCloudShellFullAccess. If CloudShell is needed, use a restricted custom policy.
1.22.evidence_template: AWSCloudShellFullAccess policy attachment was verified via ListEntitiesForPolicy. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.1.1.title: Ensure S3 Bucket Policy is set to deny HTTP requests
2.1.1.description: S3 bucket policies should include an explicit Deny for requests where aws:SecureTransport is false, enforcing HTTPS-only access.
2.1.1.evidence_template: S3 bucket policies were analyzed for aws:SecureTransport deny statements via GetBucketPolicy. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.1.2.title: Ensure MFA Delete is enabled on S3 buckets
2.1.2.description: MFA Delete requires additional authentication before permanently deleting S3 objects or changing versioning state.
2.1.2.manual_steps: MFA Delete can only be enabled by the root account. Verify via GetBucketVersioning.
2.1.2.evidence_template: S3 bucket MFA Delete status was checked via GetBucketVersioning. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.1.3.title: Ensure all data in Amazon S3 has been discovered, classified and secured when required
2.1.3.description: Use Amazon Macie to discover and classify sensitive data in S3 buckets.
2.1.3.manual_steps: Enable Amazon Macie and review S3 data classification results. Ensure sensitive data buckets have appropriate access controls.
2.1.3.evidence_template: Control 2.1.3 requires manual review of Macie data classification.

2.1.4.title: Ensure that S3 Buckets are configured with 'Block public access (bucket settings)'
2.1.4.description: All four S3 Block Public Access settings should be enabled to prevent unintended public access to bucket contents.
2.1.4.evidence_template: S3 Block Public Access settings were verified via GetPublicAccessBlock (account and bucket level). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.2.1.title: Ensure EBS Volume Encryption is Enabled in all Regions
2.2.1.description: EBS default encryption ensures all new EBS volumes are encrypted automatically without requiring per-volume configuration.
2.2.1.evidence_template: EBS default encryption status was verified per region via GetEbsEncryptionByDefault. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.3.1.title: Ensure that encryption-at-rest is enabled for RDS Instances
2.3.1.description: RDS instances should have encryption at rest enabled to protect stored data.
2.3.1.evidence_template: RDS instance encryption status was verified via DescribeDBInstances (StorageEncrypted). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.3.2.title: Ensure Auto Minor Version Upgrade feature is Enabled for RDS Instances
2.3.2.description: Auto minor version upgrade ensures RDS instances receive security patches automatically.
2.3.2.evidence_template: RDS auto minor version upgrade was verified via DescribeDBInstances (AutoMinorVersionUpgrade). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.3.3.title: Ensure that public access is not given to RDS Instance
2.3.3.description: RDS instances should not be publicly accessible from the internet.
2.3.3.evidence_template: RDS public accessibility was verified via DescribeDBInstances (PubliclyAccessible). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

2.4.1.title: Ensure that encryption is enabled for EFS file systems
2.4.1.description: EFS file systems should be encrypted at rest. Encryption can only be enabled at creation time.
2.4.1.manual_steps: For unencrypted EFS: create a new encrypted file system, migrate data, then delete the old one.
2.4.1.evidence_template: EFS encryption status was verified via DescribeFileSystems (Encrypted). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.1.title: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
3.1.description: CloudTrail should be enabled with multi-region logging to capture API activity across all AWS regions.
3.1.evidence_template: Multi-region CloudTrail trail existence was verified via DescribeTrails. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.2.title: Ensure CloudTrail log file validation is enabled
3.2.description: Log file validation creates digitally signed digest files for log integrity verification.
3.2.evidence_template: CloudTrail log file validation was verified via DescribeTrails (LogFileValidationEnabled). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.3.title: Ensure AWS Config is enabled in all regions
3.3.description: AWS Config records resource configurations and changes. It should be enabled in all regions for complete visibility.
3.3.evidence_template: AWS Config recorder status was verified via DescribeConfigurationRecorders and DescribeConfigurationRecorderStatus. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.4.title: Ensure S3 bucket access logging is enabled on the CloudTrail S3 bucket
3.4.description: Server access logging on the CloudTrail S3 bucket enables tracking of access requests to audit log files.
3.4.evidence_template: CloudTrail S3 bucket access logging was verified via GetBucketLogging. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.5.title: Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted at rest using KMS CMKs
3.5.description: CloudTrail logs should be encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key for additional control over log encryption.
3.5.evidence_template: CloudTrail KMS encryption was verified via DescribeTrails (KmsKeyId). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.6.title: Ensure rotation for customer-created symmetric CMKs is enabled
3.6.description: KMS key rotation ensures that encryption keys are automatically rotated annually.
3.6.evidence_template: KMS key rotation was verified via GetKeyRotationStatus for customer-managed symmetric CMKs. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.7.title: Ensure VPC flow logging is enabled in all VPCs
3.7.description: VPC Flow Logs capture network traffic metadata for security analysis and incident response.
3.7.evidence_template: VPC flow log status was verified via DescribeFlowLogs for all VPCs. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.8.title: Ensure that Object-level logging for write events is enabled for S3 bucket
3.8.description: S3 object-level write event logging in CloudTrail enables tracking of data modifications (PutObject, DeleteObject).
3.8.evidence_template: S3 object-level write event logging was verified via GetEventSelectors on multi-region trails. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

3.9.title: Ensure that Object-level logging for read events is enabled for S3 bucket
3.9.description: S3 object-level read event logging in CloudTrail enables tracking of data access (GetObject).
3.9.evidence_template: S3 object-level read event logging was verified via GetEventSelectors on multi-region trails. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.1.title: Ensure unauthorized API calls are monitored
4.1.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect unauthorized API calls (AccessDenied, UnauthorizedAccess errors).
4.1.manual_steps: Verify the metric filter pattern matches unauthorized access error codes and the alarm has an SNS topic configured.
4.1.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for unauthorized API calls was verified on CloudTrail log group. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.2.title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored
4.2.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect console logins without MFA.
4.2.manual_steps: Verify the metric filter pattern checks for ConsoleLogin events where MFAUsed != Yes.
4.2.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for console sign-in without MFA was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.3.title: Ensure usage of 'root' account is monitored
4.3.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect any usage of the root account.
4.3.manual_steps: Verify the metric filter pattern matches root user identity type and the alarm sends to an active SNS topic.
4.3.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for root account usage was verified on CloudTrail log group. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.4.title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored
4.4.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect IAM policy changes (CreatePolicy, DeletePolicy, AttachRolePolicy, etc.).
4.4.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers all IAM policy change API calls.
4.4.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for IAM policy changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.5.title: Ensure CloudTrail configuration changes are monitored
4.5.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect CloudTrail configuration changes.
4.5.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers CreateTrail, UpdateTrail, DeleteTrail, StartLogging, StopLogging.
4.5.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for CloudTrail configuration changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.6.title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored
4.6.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect console authentication failures.
4.6.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern matches ConsoleLogin events with Failed authentication.
4.6.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for console auth failures was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.7.title: Ensure disabling or scheduled deletion of customer-created CMKs is monitored
4.7.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect KMS key disabling or scheduled deletion.
4.7.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers DisableKey and ScheduleKeyDeletion from kms.amazonaws.com.
4.7.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for CMK disable/deletion was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.8.title: Ensure S3 bucket policy changes are monitored
4.8.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect S3 bucket policy changes.
4.8.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers PutBucketAcl, PutBucketPolicy, PutBucketCors, PutBucketLifecycle, PutBucketReplication, DeleteBucketPolicy, DeleteBucketCors, DeleteBucketLifecycle, DeleteBucketReplication.
4.8.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for S3 bucket policy changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.9.title: Ensure AWS Config configuration changes are monitored
4.9.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect AWS Config service configuration changes.
4.9.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers StopConfigurationRecorder, DeleteDeliveryChannel, PutDeliveryChannel, PutConfigurationRecorder.
4.9.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for Config changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.10.title: Ensure security group changes are monitored
4.10.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect security group changes.
4.10.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress/Egress, RevokeSecurityGroupIngress/Egress, CreateSecurityGroup, DeleteSecurityGroup.
4.10.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for security group changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.11.title: Ensure Network Access Control Lists (NACL) changes are monitored
4.11.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect Network ACL changes.
4.11.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers CreateNetworkAcl, CreateNetworkAclEntry, DeleteNetworkAcl, DeleteNetworkAclEntry, ReplaceNetworkAclEntry, ReplaceNetworkAclAssociation.
4.11.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for NACL changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.12.title: Ensure changes to network gateways are monitored
4.12.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect network gateway changes.
4.12.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers CreateCustomerGateway, DeleteCustomerGateway, AttachInternetGateway, CreateInternetGateway, DeleteInternetGateway, DetachInternetGateway.
4.12.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for network gateway changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.13.title: Ensure route table changes are monitored
4.13.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect route table changes.
4.13.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers CreateRoute, CreateRouteTable, ReplaceRoute, ReplaceRouteTableAssociation, DeleteRouteTable, DeleteRoute, DisassociateRouteTable.
4.13.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for route table changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.14.title: Ensure VPC changes are monitored
4.14.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect VPC changes.
4.14.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers CreateVpc, DeleteVpc, ModifyVpcAttribute, AcceptVpcPeeringConnection, CreateVpcPeeringConnection, DeleteVpcPeeringConnection, RejectVpcPeeringConnection, AttachClassicLinkVpc, DetachClassicLinkVpc, DisableVpcClassicLink, EnableVpcClassicLink.
4.14.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for VPC changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.15.title: Ensure AWS Organizations changes are monitored
4.15.description: A CloudWatch metric filter and alarm should detect AWS Organizations changes in the master account.
4.15.manual_steps: Verify metric filter pattern covers organizations.amazonaws.com events and alarm is in the master/management account.
4.15.evidence_template: CloudWatch metric filter for Organizations changes was verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

4.16.title: Ensure AWS Security Hub is enabled
4.16.description: AWS Security Hub provides a comprehensive view of security findings from multiple AWS services and partner tools.
4.16.evidence_template: Security Hub status was verified via DescribeHub. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

5.1.title: Ensure no Network ACLs allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
5.1.description: Network ACLs should not allow unrestricted inbound access to SSH (22) or RDP (3389) ports from the internet.
5.1.evidence_template: Network ACLs were scanned for inbound rules allowing 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to ports 22/3389. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

5.2.title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
5.2.description: Security groups should not allow unrestricted inbound access from 0.0.0.0/0 to SSH (22) or RDP (3389).
5.2.evidence_template: Security groups were scanned for IPv4 0.0.0.0/0 ingress to admin ports (22, 3389). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

5.3.title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from ::/0 to remote server administration ports
5.3.description: Security groups should not allow unrestricted inbound access from IPv6 ::/0 to SSH (22) or RDP (3389).
5.3.evidence_template: Security groups were scanned for IPv6 ::/0 ingress to admin ports (22, 3389). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

5.4.title: Ensure the default security group of every VPC restricts all traffic
5.4.description: The default security group should have no inbound or outbound rules to prevent resources launched without a specific SG from having network access.
5.4.evidence_template: Default security groups were checked for active ingress/egress rules via DescribeSecurityGroups. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

5.5.title: Ensure routing tables for VPC peering are 'least access'
5.5.description: Route tables for VPC peering should use specific CIDR ranges, not overly broad routes.
5.5.manual_steps: Review VPC peering route table entries. Ensure routes to peering connections use specific CIDRs, not 0.0.0.0/0 or the entire peer VPC CIDR.
5.5.evidence_template: Control 5.5 requires manual review of VPC peering route table entries.

5.6.title: Ensure that EC2 Metadata Service only allows IMDSv2
5.6.description: EC2 Instance Metadata Service v2 (IMDSv2) uses session-based tokens and mitigates SSRF-based credential theft attacks.
5.6.evidence_template: EC2 instance metadata service version was verified via DescribeInstances (MetadataOptions.HttpTokens). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}
